programme.desc: The long cross-examination of Yazeed Essa's alleged fixer was not over yet. When investigators said they began closing in on him as a suspect, Yazeed suddenly left the country. It was Jamal Khalife who said he helped him. Khalife claimed to have helped Yazeed Essa hide from U.S. officials and lie to police about his situation. programme.desc: In August 1992, a man returned to his Memphis home and discovered that his wife was missing. The husband, his friends, family and neighbors began to receive ransom calls.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: The Los Macheteros terrorist organization sought to end U.S. rule of Puerto Rico through violence. When the group claimed responsibility for an attack on 18 unarmed servicemen traveling near San Juan, the FBI is called in to investigate the killers.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In January 1984, a U.S. Customs officer disappeared from his post near the Del Rio, Texas border crossing, prompting local police to search for four Latin American suspects. The following day, the official was found dead.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: On July 4, 1987, seven inmates broke out of a high-security prison in New Mexico. The daring escape was orchestrated by convicted murderer William Wayne Gilbert, who was serving a life sentence.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In October 1993, a robber surprised unsuspecting customers by dropping through the roof of a California bank.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In 1984, the son of a successful real estate developer was kidnapped in Miami. After the father received a $3 million ransom demand from a self-proclaimed terrorist, the FBI became involved and agents began to suspect a pair of Colombian nationals.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: The story of a terrifying killer who snatched away the lives of four victims before being caught while trying to claim his fifth.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Prisoners
programme.desc: While on trial for rape, 33-year-old Brian Nichols goes on a violent rampage that results in three murders.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Prisoners
programme.desc: Gary Vinter, in prison for double murder, stabs a fellow inmate in the eye and gives another brain damage.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Prisoners
programme.desc: Nothing was going to stop Ricky Dubose from breaking free of prison. Whilst being transported on a bus between facilities, he killed two correctional officers before escaping - and going on the run.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Prisoners
programme.desc: James Robertson is one of the most feared men in the US prison system; he murdered his cellmate, attacked a prison employee and doesn't hold back from getting what he wants.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: Over a 25-year career of murder, Richard Biegenwald killed at least six people, but he is suspected of many more murders. Imprisoned in 1958 aged just 18 for shooting dead a grocery store owner, Biegenwald was released 17 years later.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: A look into the case of a sadistic killer who posed as an MI5 agent in order to torture and murder his victim.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: The chilling story of the serial killer who worked as a nurse in Texas, where she killed many children and infants by injecting them with drugs.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: The story of 27-year-old Pawel Relowicz, who raped and killed 21-year-old Hull student Libby Squire in a case that made headlines around the UK.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: An investigation into the woman who was driven into a jealous rage and stabbed her sister to death while her children slept upstairs.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: The story of a killer so evil that not even her own son could escape her clutches. She became the first woman to be executed in Florida since 1848.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: In January 1973, 20-year-old mother of one Dawn Magyar vanished while on a shopping trip in Owosso, Michigan.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: Ali Qazimaj is an Albanian conman who brutally murdered an elderly couple in 2016.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: Maria's marriage crumbled when Malcolm discovered her bigamy. As he moved on and found new love, Maria's jealousy erupted into dangerous rage, leading to a confrontation that exposed how far she was willing to go.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: Stephen Farrow committed two murders in early 2012 motivated by an obsessive hatred of Christianity, which he attributed to sexual abuse from a priest.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: The story of the I-95 killer, who brutally killed six men in 1994 before being executed three years later.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: The chilling story of Mark Martin, who murdered three homeless women in his quest to become Nottingham's first serial killer.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: Billy Mansfield Jr. moved between Florida and Michigan, targeting vulnerable women and girls. Between 1975 and 1980, Mansfield Jr. murdered at least five victims, burying four of them at his family home in Spring Hill, Florida.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Prisoners
programme.desc: One of the UK's most infamous serial killers, Joanna Dennehy brutally stabbed three men to death.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Prisoners
programme.desc: Edward Johnson is sent to prison for the brutal murder of a female friend, but the violence doesn't end there.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A suspicious fire swept through an apartment killing two young women. A closer look at the fire scene revealed something hidden in the ashes.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 1981, six-year-old Cassie Hansen disappeared from her St. Paul, Minnesota church during an evening service.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A husband confesses to his wife's murder, only to be found not guilty a year later when a phone tip and forensic evidence point to another.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Edward Honaker was sentenced to three life terms in prison, but he steadfastly maintained his innocence.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: The wife of a murdered horse rancher explains that the last time she saw her husband was when he was leaving the house to go jogging. Police become suspicious when forensic evidence eventually leads investigators back to the ranch.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: After a street fight takes the life of a national wrestling champion, police must determine if he was killed in cold blood or in self-defense.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 1981, New York correctional officer Donna Payant disappeared and was later found in a landfill. The medical examiner not only identified the cause of death but also found an important clue.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 1992, Laura Houghteling disappeared from her Bethesda home and was never seen again. Laura's bedroom was searched and forensic science was used to direct them a to prime suspect.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: An infant was rushed to the emergency room with serious breathing problems. Within months, there were more than 30 cases. Doctors searched frantically for the cause and a cure.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When Chris Ochoa confessed to the 1988 rape and murder of Nancy DePriest, the case was closed. 8 years later, Achim Marino confessed to the same crime. Can DNA evidence identify the real perpetrator?

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Police charge a mentally unstable man with his wife's murder, but a medical examiner discovers that she died of natural causes.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Almost a hundred vehicles were involved in the single most deadly automobile accident in American history.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: For 15 months, a serial killer was strangling prostitutes in Florida then taunting police by leaving the bodies in plain sight. The only clues were a tire impression and some threads.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Residents of Noel, Missouri were stunned to learn that their bank had been robbed and the bank president was missing. His body was later found floating in a lake, securely bound to a chair with duct tape.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Police exhume the body of Karla Brown, murdered in her home in 1978, to study bite wounds, which eventually lead to the killer.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 1991, a high school girl vanished after attending a party. The prime suspect had two alibi witnesses for the night she disappeared. A solid alibi can often overcome circumstantial evidence, but forensic evidence is another matter.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Survivors of Nazi atrocities recognized Archbishop Trifa as having led an attack against Jews during WWII.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When a pipe bomb rips through a rural home, killing a young man and seriously injuring his mother, detectives hope that the remnants of a mailing label will lead them to a killer.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 1984, a passerby finds the torso of a woman in a cardboard box. A forensic entomologist analyzes nearby insect activity to determine when the victim was killed.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When a Pennsylvania man checks into a hospital for leg pain and later dies of thallium poisoning, police investigate his wife and find her in the process of collecting an insurance payment for her previous husband's death.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: At a murder scene, investigators find a shoe-print, several foreign hairs, and unknown fingerprints. But they believe the most telling piece of evidence is the bite mark on the victim.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A 1995 fire razes the mansion of a Kansas doctor, killing two of her three children; disturbing accounts from an ex-husband lead investigators to believe the doctor set the blaze herself.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 1962, the people of the small town of Hanford, California lost their sense of peace when one of their own, 15-year-old Marlene Miller, was murdered.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Time of death becomes pivotal after a pregnant woman is found murdered in her air-conditioned bedroom.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When police in the Great Plains are called about a dead body, they do a background check on the victim.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 1995, when off-duty Maine State Trooper Vicky Gardner is attacked by Steven Fortin during a routine stop, it triggers a chain of events which jumpstarts a stalled murder case in New Jersey.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 1984, California firefighters battled ten arson fires in three weeks. When cigarettes and a scrap of paper connected the case to similar fires further north, the hunt was on for a dangerous pyromaniac.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A man tells police he shot an intruder who killed his wife. Forensic evidence disproves that story later on.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Police are led on a wild goose chase when the wife of a prominent banker is kidnapped. An entomologist's unexpected discovery leads to a new suspect - and the realization that ordinary office equipment can leave extraordinary clues.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A woman who suffered from mental illness is found dead. Was it due to natural causes, suicide or murder?

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Two men called police to report the same murder. The investigation uncovered more unusual circumstances.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: After a street fight takes the life of a national wrestling champion, police must determine if he was killed in cold blood or in self-defense.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: After a day of fishing in a small, quiet village in Switzerland, a teenage boy did not return home as planned. The investigation revealed some important microscopic evidence in the water near where he was last seen.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: The body of 16-year-old Leanne Tiernan was discovered nine months after she disappeared. Forensic scientists found clues that painted a virtual portrait of her killer.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When a serial killer was terrorizing the city of St. Louis, the FBI didn't need lab tests, fingerprints or any of the usual methods. All they needed was a computer.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Forensic computer analysis, blood spatter analysis and a painstaking investigation lead police to a lawyer husband Michael "Mick" Fletcher with a motive to kill his wife since he was having a sexual affair with judge Susan Chrzanowski.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When Rosemary Anderson is found dead on the side of the road, her boyfriend becomes the prime suspect. He eventually confessed to her murder, but so did another man - serial killer, Eric Edgar Cooke.

programme.desc: The search of a vehicle reveals drugs and a handgun. A homeless person trashes a woman's house.

programme.desc: A Special Enforcement Detail officer executes a plan to catch some burglars who ransacked several homes. A 9-1-1 caller reports that a woman broke into her car.

programme.desc: A car passing through a Lake Tahoe agriculture checkpoint registers as stolen, resulting in a chase. A vehicle with an expired registration is seen weaving in and out of their lane. A car is pulled over for a defective tail light.

programme.desc: The investigation into a case of domestic abuse leads to the arrest of the abuser's friend on a $25,000 warrant. A deputy investigates two women who are parked behind a closed business late at night.

programme.desc: A deputy responds to a report of a dispute and finds a house destroyed by an intoxicated person.

programme.desc: A bear is reported to be wandering near people's homes. Deputies respond to a domestic violence call.

programme.desc: A deputy responds to a report of assault with a bat and pepper spray. Illicit drug use is reported to be going on in a feed store parking lot.

programme.desc: A traffic stop reveals a man with stolen food and burglary tools who drives without a license. A deputy responds to a call about two intoxicated men. Two men are reportedly fighting at a department store.

programme.title: Sheriffs: El Dorado County
programme.desc: Police seek to take down a known drug house where they expect many arrests. Deputies investigate a van parked after hours in a school parking area. Knives, bullets and marijuana are found in a suspicious car.

programme.desc: Deputies search a house for a domestic violence offender and his wife is pretending not to know his location; a probation search on a repeat offender.

programme.desc: While patrolling an area after a string of recent burglaries, a deputy finds a wanted man. A deputy is alerted to the location of two men with drugs firing off their guns.

programme.desc: A boater is reported to be under the influence of drugs or alcohol. Open containers and drugs are found in a suspicious vehicle. Deputies investigate individuals stealing recycling material.

programme.desc: An undercover narcotics team goes after a drug house. A drunk motorist drives over a cliff and into a river.
